machine.name: Service Intake Assessment Monitor
machine.description: Monitors intake and needs assessment workflow for community service recipients — tracking need severity, functional status, safety risk, and support network adequacy. Determines intake priority and routing for case assignment. In community services, intake triage determines whether a person receives immediate crisis response, intensive case management, or standard program enrollment. Output at [956:960] feeds the Enrollment Coordination Monitor.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: New community service applicants; crisis walk-ins; referrals from partner agencies; individuals experiencing housing, food, or safety crises

sensorNormalization.need_severity_norm: 0.0=acute multi-domain crisis (shelter, food, safety all failing simultaneously), 0.5=significant need in one domain; manageable with standard services, 1.0=moderate need with adequate existing support; standard enrollment appropriate
sensorNormalization.functional_status_norm: 0.0=unable to complete intake independently; cognitive, physical, or psychiatric barriers to participation, 0.5=needs assistance with parts of intake; low literacy or language barrier, 1.0=fully capable of completing intake process independently
sensorNormalization.safety_risk_norm: 0.0=imminent safety risk (DV, child abuse, elder abuse, suicidality reported), 0.5=elevated risk factors present but not acute (past DV, unstable housing, substance use), 1.0=no safety concerns identified during screening
sensorNormalization.support_network_norm: 0.0=completely isolated; no family, friends, or existing services engaged, 0.5=limited support (one informal contact; partial service engagement), 1.0=strong support network; family engaged; existing services active

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="community-services".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning ServiceIntakeAssessmentMonitor matures.

rules.description: PRIORITY_INTAKE: acute crisis requiring immediate case worker assignment at [956:960]
COMPLEX_INTAKE: multi-domain needs requiring intensive case management assignment at [956:960]
INTAKE_COMPLETE: needs assessment finished; ready for program enrollment at [956:960]

sequences.name: Crisis Intake: STANDARD+ → COMPLEX → PRIORITY → PRIORITY_INTAKE

metadata.description: Tracks intake complexity escalation from a standard baseline. In community services, initial assessment sometimes reveals acute safety risks or multi-domain crises that require immediate priority routing. The STANDARD+ baseline ensures at least one standard intake before crisis escalation can trigger — preventing initial crisis walk-ins from being misrouted through this sequence.

metadata.name: STANDARD
metadata.description: STANDARD+ — moderate need; functional for intake; no acute safety concerns; some support present. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: COMPLEX
metadata.description: COMPLEX — multiple need domains; functional barriers requiring intake support; risk factors present.
metadata.need_severity: 0.25–0.45 (two or more need domains; requires complex case management)

metadata.name: PRIORITY
metadata.description: PRIORITY — acute multi-domain crisis; significant functional barriers; active safety risk. Fires PRIORITY_INTAKE for immediate crisis response routing.
PRIORITY_INTAKE: acute crisis requiring immediate case worker assignment at [956:960]
metadata.action: Immediate caseworker assignment; safety plan activation; partner agency crisis notification; same-day service initiation

metadata.description: Fires COMPLEX_INTAKE for applicants with multi-domain needs or functional barriers who require intensive case management routing rather than standard program enrollment. These cases need a dedicated case manager, not just a benefits referral.

metadata.name: COMPLEX_STABLE
metadata.description: COMPLEX_STABLE+ — significant need severity; functional barriers requiring intake support; no acute safety risk; limited support. isInitial A*A = A+: fires COMPLEX_INTAKE each cycle.
metadata.scenario: Elderly applicant with cognitive decline; needs housing, meals, and health aide; cannot complete intake independently but not in acute danger

metadata.description: COMPLEX_INTAKE: multi-domain needs requiring intensive case management assignment at [956:960]
metadata.action: Complex case manager assignment; multi-agency coordination plan; functional capacity assessment; care plan development

metadata.description: Fires INTAKE_COMPLETE when needs have been assessed, functional barriers addressed, safety screening is clear, and support network is confirmed — ready for program enrollment and case assignment. Downstream enrollment coordination monitor reads this alongside eligibility confirmation.

metadata.name: RESOLVED
metadata.description: RESOLVED+ — needs assessed; functional barriers cleared; safety screening clear; support network identified. Fires INTAKE_COMPLETE each cycle.
metadata.scenario: Applicant completed intake with navigator support; safety screened clear; connected to case manager; ready for enrollment

metadata.description: INTAKE_COMPLETE: needs assessment finished; ready for program enrollment at [956:960]
metadata.action: Enrollment coordination monitor receives INTAKE_COMPLETE; case assignment queue populated

inputSequences.name: Domestic Violence Survivor — Priority Intake
inputSequences.pattern: 5-step: STANDARD→STANDARD→COMPLEX (DV disclosed)→PRIORITY (imminent risk identified)→RESOLVED (after safety plan)
inputSequences.description: New housing assistance applicant discloses DV during intake; safety risk elevated; priority crisis services engaged; safety plan stabilizes situation; intake completed.
